description: Landing page loads and displays product information for GymAccess

Step 10: Verify all entered fields are persisted correctly

Step 2: Fill in name, email, and phone

description: Signup page validates email format

Step 2: Enter invalid email 'notanemail'
Step 3: Try to submit
Step 4: Verify email validation error is shown

description: Signup page validates required fields
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page
Step 2: Submit form without any data
Step 3: Verify validation error for name
Step 4: Verify validation error for email
Step 5: Verify validation error for terms acceptance

description: Stripe checkout session is created when member proceeds to payment
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page
Step 2: Complete signup form with valid data
Step 3: Accept terms and proceed
Step 4: Verify Stripe checkout session is created via API
Step 5: Verify the checkout session has correct price amount
Step 6: Verify the checkout session has correct billing interval

description: Successful Stripe payment activates membership via webhook
steps: Step 1: Create a member through signup flow
Step 2: Simulate Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes from 'Pending' to 'Active'
Step 4: Verify subscription record is created
Step 5: Verify subscription status is 'active'
Step 6: Verify access code is generated for the member
Step 7: Verify welcome notification is triggered

description: Stripe webhook endpoint accepts and verifies webhook signatures
steps: Step 1: Send POST to http://localhost:8080/webhooks/stripe with valid signature
Step 2: Verify 200 response
Step 3: Send POST with invalid signature
Step 4: Verify 400 or 401 response

description: Payment failure webhook transitions member to PastDue status
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_failed webhook from Stripe
Step 3: Verify member status changes to 'PastDue'
Step 4: Verify payment failure notification is triggered

description: Subscription cancellation webhook transitions member to Cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate customer.subscription.deleted webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes to 'Cancelled'
Step 4: Verify access code is revoked
Step 5: Verify cancellation notification is triggered

description: Subscription renewal webhook keeps member Active
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_succeeded webhook
Step 3: Verify member status remains 'Active'
Step 4: Verify payment receipt notification is triggered

description: PastDue member regains Active status on successful payment retry
steps: Step 1: Create a member with PastDue status
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_succeeded webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes back to 'Active'
Step 4: Verify access code is regenerated or reactivated

description: Member status transitions follow valid state machine (Pending->Active->PastDue->Cancelled/Expired)
steps: Step 1: Create member, verify status is 'Pending'
Step 2: Activate via webhook, verify status is 'Active'
Step 3: Fail payment via webhook, verify status is 'PastDue'
Step 4: Cancel subscription via webhook, verify status is 'Cancelled'
Step 5: Verify that invalid transitions are rejected (e.g., Pending->Cancelled directly)

description: Shared PIN access code is assigned to all active members of a gym
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with 'Shared PIN' access type
Step 2: Set gym PIN to '4821'
Step 3: Create and activate member 1
Step 4: Verify member 1 receives PIN '4821'
Step 5: Create and activate member 2
Step 6: Verify member 2 receives same PIN '4821'
Step 7: Verify both members see the same code

description: Individual PIN is generated uniquely per member
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with 'Individual PIN' access type
Step 2: Create and activate member 1
Step 3: Verify member 1 receives a unique PIN
Step 4: Create and activate member 2
Step 5: Verify member 2 receives a different PIN than member 1
Step 6: Verify both PINs are of the configured length

description: Access code is generated automatically when membership becomes active
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Individual PIN access type
Step 2: Create a member (status Pending, no access code)
Step 3: Activate membership via payment webhook
Step 4: Verify access code record is created
Step 5: Verify access code has valid_from set to now
Step 6: Verify access code is stored in the database

description: Access code is revoked when membership is cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with an access code
Step 2: Cancel the membership via webhook
Step 3: Verify the access code valid_to is set
Step 4: Verify the member no longer has a valid access code

description: Admin can rotate the shared PIN for a gym
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Access section of admin dashboard
Step 3: Verify current shared PIN is displayed
Step 4: Click 'Rotate PIN' button
Step 5: Verify new PIN is generated and displayed
Step 6: Verify old PIN is no longer valid
Step 7: Verify all active members are notified of new PIN

steps: Step 1: Set ACCESS_CODE_LENGTH to 6 in environment
Step 2: Create a gym with Individual PIN
Step 3: Create and activate a member
Step 4: Verify generated PIN has exactly 6 digits

description: Welcome email is sent when membership is created and activated
steps: Step 1: Create and activate a new member
Step 2: Verify welcome email notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify email contains gym name

Step 5: Verify email is sent to the member's email address

description: Payment failure reminder is sent when payment fails
steps: Step 1: Trigger a payment failure webhook

description: Cancellation notification is sent when membership is cancelled
steps: Step 1: Cancel an active membership

description: Access code notification is sent when code is generated

description: Full automation flow: signup -> payment -> activation -> access code -> notification
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Shared PIN access
Step 2: Navigate to public signup page
Step 3: Fill in member details
Step 4: Accept terms
Step 5: Proceed to payment
Step 6: Simulate successful Stripe payment webhook
Step 7: Verify member status is Active
Step 8: Verify access code is generated
Step 9: Verify welcome email is sent with access code
Step 10: Verify member appears in admin members list as Active
Step 11: Verify payment appears in admin payments section

description: Full automation flow with Individual PIN: unique code per member

Step 2: Sign up member 1 and activate

Step 4: Sign up member 2 and activate
Step 5: Verify member 2 gets a different unique PIN
Step 6: Verify admin access section shows both individual PINs
Step 7: Cancel member 1's subscription

Step 10: Verify notifications sent at each step

description: Full membership lifecycle: Pending -> Active -> PastDue -> Active -> Cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create member via signup (status: Pending)
Step 2: Process payment webhook (status: Active)

Step 4: Process payment failure webhook (status: PastDue)
Step 5: Verify access code revoked or flagged
Step 6: Process successful payment retry webhook (status: Active)
Step 7: Verify access code restored
Step 8: Process cancellation webhook (status: Cancelled)
Step 9: Verify access code permanently revoked
Step 10: Verify all state transitions logged
Step 11: Verify notifications sent for each transition

description: Complete gym onboarding: admin registers, configures gym, shares signup link, first member joins
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin registration page
Step 2: Enter admin name, email, and password
Step 3: Enter gym name 'Fjord Fitness'
Step 4: Enter gym location 'Bergen, Norway'
Step 5: Select access type 'Shared PIN'
Step 6: Enter membership price 349
Step 7: Select monthly billing
Step 8: Submit registration
Step 9: Verify admin is logged in to dashboard
Step 10: Navigate to Settings and verify gym details are correct
Step 11: Copy the public signup link from dashboard
Step 12: Open signup link in incognito/new browser
Step 13: Verify gym name and price are shown on signup page

description: Admin manages multiple members through their full lifecycle
steps: Step 1: Set up gym with Individual PIN access
Step 2: Sign up Member A and activate via webhook
Step 3: Sign up Member B and activate via webhook
Step 4: Sign up Member C (leave as Pending)
Step 5: Log in as admin and verify 2 Active, 1 Pending members
Step 6: Suspend Member A from admin panel
Step 7: Verify Member A status changes and PIN is revoked
Step 8: Cancel Member B from admin panel
Step 9: Verify Member B status is Cancelled and PIN is revoked
Step 10: Verify Member C is still Pending with no PIN
Step 11: Activate Member C via payment webhook
Step 12: Verify Member C now has Active status and a unique PIN

description: Payment failure and recovery flow with all notifications verified
steps: Step 1: Create gym and sign up a member
Step 2: Activate member via successful payment webhook
Step 3: Verify welcome notification sent with access code
Step 4: Simulate payment failure webhook (invoice.payment_failed)
Step 5: Verify member status changes to PastDue
Step 6: Verify payment failure notification is sent
Step 7: Verify access code is revoked or flagged
Step 8: Simulate payment retry success webhook (invoice.payment_succeeded)
Step 9: Verify member status returns to Active
Step 10: Verify new access code is generated or previous restored
Step 11: Verify payment success notification is sent
Step 12: Verify admin dashboard reflects all state changes

description: Shared PIN rotation affects all active members and sends notifications
steps: Step 1: Create gym with Shared PIN access, initial PIN 1234
Step 2: Create and activate 5 members
Step 3: Verify all 5 members have PIN 1234
Step 4: Log in as admin
Step 5: Navigate to Access section
Step 6: Verify current PIN 1234 is displayed
Step 7: Click Rotate PIN
Step 8: Verify new PIN is different from 1234
Step 9: Verify all 5 members now have the new PIN
Step 10: Verify notification was sent to all 5 members with new PIN
Step 11: Cancel one member
Step 12: Rotate PIN again
Step 13: Verify cancelled member does NOT receive notification

description: Multi-gym isolation: two gyms operate independently with separate data
steps: Step 1: Register Admin A with Gym A (Shared PIN, monthly, 299)
Step 2: Register Admin B with Gym B (Individual PIN, yearly, 2999)
Step 3: Sign up 3 members for Gym A and activate them
Step 4: Sign up 2 members for Gym B and activate them
Step 5: Log in as Admin A
Step 6: Verify only Gym A's 3 members are visible
Step 7: Verify Gym A settings show Shared PIN and 299/month
Step 8: Log out and log in as Admin B
Step 9: Verify only Gym B's 2 members are visible
Step 10: Verify Gym B settings show Individual PIN and 2999/year
Step 11: Verify Gym B members have unique individual PINs
Step 12: Verify Admin B cannot access Gym A's API endpoints

description: Full end-to-end member signup with Stripe checkout and webhook processing
steps: Step 1: Create a gym via admin registration
Step 2: Navigate to the gym's public signup page
Step 3: Verify gym name and price are displayed
Step 4: Enter member name 'Lars Hansen'
Step 5: Enter email 'lars@example.no'
Step 6: Enter phone '+4798765432'
Step 7: Accept terms and conditions
Step 8: Click proceed to payment
Step 9: Verify Stripe checkout session is created with correct amount
Step 10: Simulate checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 11: Verify member status is Active
Step 12: Verify subscription record exists with Stripe provider
Step 13: Verify access code is generated
Step 14: Verify welcome email notification contains access code

description: Admin Payments section provides accurate financial overview with multiple payment states
steps: Step 1: Create gym with monthly billing at 299 NOK
Step 2: Create and activate 5 members (5 active subscriptions)
Step 3: Fail payment for 1 member (4 active, 1 past due)
Step 4: Cancel 1 member (3 active, 1 past due, 1 cancelled)
Step 5: Log in as admin
Step 6: Navigate to Payments section
Step 7: Verify monthly revenue shows 3 * 299 = 897 NOK
Step 8: Verify active subscription count is 3
Step 9: Verify failed payment count is 1
Step 10: Verify cancelled count is tracked

description: Webhook idempotency: same webhook event processed twice does not cause duplicate actions
steps: Step 1: Create a pending member
Step 2: Send checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify member is activated
Step 4: Send the same checkout.session.completed webhook again
Step 5: Verify member is still Active (not duplicated)
Step 6: Verify only one subscription record exists
Step 7: Verify only one access code exists
Step 8: Verify only one welcome notification was sent
Step 9: Verify API returns 200 (not error) for duplicate webhook
Step 10: Verify no duplicate database entries

description: Database seed creates a complete demo environment for testing
steps: Step 1: Run docker compose up with fresh database
Step 2: Run ./scripts/seed
Step 3: Verify at least one demo gym is created
Step 4: Verify demo admin account exists
Step 5: Verify demo members exist in Active, Pending, Cancelled states
Step 6: Verify demo subscriptions are linked to members
Step 7: Verify demo access codes exist for active members
Step 8: Log in with demo admin credentials

Step 10: Navigate to Members and verify demo members are listed

description: Admin member detail page shows comprehensive member information

description: Individual PIN gym: access codes lifecycle through member states
steps: Step 1: Create gym with Individual PIN and ACCESS_CODE_LENGTH=4
Step 2: Create and activate Member A
Step 3: Verify Member A receives a 4-digit unique PIN
Step 4: Record Member A's PIN
Step 5: Fail Member A's payment (status -> PastDue)
Step 6: Verify Member A's PIN is revoked
Step 7: Retry payment successfully (status -> Active)
Step 8: Verify Member A receives a new or restored PIN
Step 9: Cancel Member A (status -> Cancelled)
Step 10: Verify Member A's PIN is permanently revoked
Step 11: Verify no valid access code exists for Member A

description: Admin settings update propagates to signup page and future members

description: API authentication and authorization comprehensive test
steps: Step 1: Call GET /admin/members without auth header
Step 2: Verify 401 response
Step 3: Call GET /admin/members with invalid token
Step 4: Verify 401 response
Step 5: Log in as Admin A and get auth token
Step 6: Call GET /admin/members with Admin A token
Step 7: Verify 200 response with Admin A's gym members only
Step 8: Call GET /admin/members/{gym-b-member-id} with Admin A token
Step 9: Verify 403 or 404 (Admin A cannot see Gym B members)
Step 10: Call public endpoint POST /members (signup) without auth
Step 11: Verify public endpoint works without auth

description: Docker Compose development environment full setup and verification
steps: Step 1: Verify docker-compose.yml exists
Step 2: Verify docker-compose.dev.yml exists
Step 3: Verify .env.example exists with all required variables
Step 4: Copy .env.example to .env
Step 5: Run docker compose up
Step 6: Verify frontend is accessible at localhost:3000
Step 7: Verify API is accessible at localhost:8080
Step 8: Verify PostgreSQL is running and accepting connections
Step 9: Verify database tables are created via migrations
Step 10: Run seed script and verify demo data is loaded
Step 11: Log in with demo credentials and verify dashboard works

description: Database migration adds Stripe Connect fields to gyms table (stripe_connect_account_id, stripe_connect_status)
steps: Step 1: Run database migration
Step 2: Verify gyms table has stripe_connect_account_id column (nullable text)
Step 3: Verify gyms table has stripe_connect_status column with default 'not_connected'
Step 4: Verify stripe_connect_status accepts values: 'not_connected', 'pending', 'active'
Step 5: Verify existing gyms default to stripe_connect_status = 'not_connected'
Step 6: Verify migration is idempotent (running twice does not fail)

description: POST /admin/stripe/connect creates a Stripe Express connected account and returns onboarding URL
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin and obtain JWT
Step 2: POST /admin/stripe/connect
Step 3: Verify 200 response with { url: '...', accountId: '...' }
Step 4: Verify a Stripe Express account is created via Stripe API
Step 5: Verify gym's stripe_connect_account_id is saved in DB
Step 6: Verify gym's stripe_connect_status is set to 'pending'
Step 7: Verify the URL is a valid Stripe account onboarding link
Step 8: Verify return_url points back to admin settings page
Step 9: Verify refresh_url points back to admin settings page

description: Stripe account.updated webhook sets gym connect status to 'active' when onboarding completes
steps: Step 1: Create gym with stripe_connect_account_id set and stripe_connect_status='pending'
Step 2: POST /webhooks/stripe with account.updated event where charges_enabled=true and payouts_enabled=true, with 'account' field matching gym's connected account ID
Step 3: Verify gym's stripe_connect_status is updated to 'active'
Step 4: POST /webhooks/stripe with account.updated where charges_enabled=false
Step 5: Verify gym's stripe_connect_status stays 'pending' (not yet active)
Step 6: Verify webhook returns 200 for both cases

description: Admin Settings page shows Stripe Connect status with state-appropriate UI
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin for gym with stripe_connect_status='not_connected'
Step 2: Navigate to Settings page
Step 3: Verify 'Stripe Connect' section is present
Step 4: Verify status shows 'Not Connected' with a 'Connect with Stripe' button
Step 5: Update gym to stripe_connect_status='pending'
Step 6: Reload Settings page
Step 7: Verify status shows 'Pending — complete Stripe onboarding' with re-initiate button
Step 8: Update gym to stripe_connect_status='active'
Step 9: Reload Settings page
Step 10: Verify status shows 'Connected' with a green indicator and no Connect button

description: Stripe checkout session routes gym member payment to gym's connected Stripe account with platform fee
steps: Step 1: Set gym's stripe_connect_account_id to a connected account ID and stripe_connect_status='active'
Step 2: Set PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT=5 in environment
Step 3: Create a member for this gym
Step 4: POST /subscriptions/checkout with memberId
Step 5: Verify Stripe checkout session has transfer_data.destination set to the gym's connected account ID
Step 6: Verify checkout session has application_fee_amount equal to 5% of membership_price in smallest currency unit
Step 7: Verify checkout session does NOT have on_behalf_of set to platform account

description: PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT environment variable controls the platform fee on gym member payments
steps: Step 1: Set PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT=10 in .env and restart API
Step 2: Create gym with active Connect and membership_price=300
Step 3: POST /subscriptions/checkout
Step 4: Verify application_fee_amount = 3000 (10% of 300 NOK expressed in øre)
Step 5: Set PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT=0 and restart API
Step 6: POST /subscriptions/checkout
Step 7: Verify application_fee_amount is 0 or not set
Step 8: Verify PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT is documented in .env.example with a comment

description: Checkout returns an error when gym does not have an active Stripe Connect account
steps: Step 1: Create gym with stripe_connect_status='not_connected'
Step 2: Create a member for this gym
Step 3: POST /subscriptions/checkout with memberId
Step 4: Verify response is 400 or 503 with descriptive error message
Step 5: Verify error message indicates the gym owner must connect their Stripe account
Step 6: Verify no Stripe checkout session is created
Step 7: Repeat for stripe_connect_status='pending' — verify same error

description: Webhook handler routes events to correct handler: platform account events vs connected account events
steps: Step 1: POST /webhooks/stripe with invoice.payment_succeeded event without 'account' field (platform SaaS event)
Step 2: Verify handler treats this as a SaaS billing event and updates gym.saas_status
Step 3: POST /webhooks/stripe with invoice.payment_succeeded with 'account' field set to gym's connected account ID (gym member payment event)
Step 4: Verify handler treats this as a member payment event and updates member status
Step 5: Verify no cross-contamination between the two event routing paths
Step 6: Verify both return 200

description: Database migration adds SaaS subscription fields to gyms table (saas_status, saas_subscription_id, saas_stripe_customer_id, trial_ends_at)
steps: Step 1: Run database migration
Step 2: Verify gyms table has saas_status column defaulting to 'trial'
Step 3: Verify saas_status accepts values: 'trial', 'active', 'past_due', 'cancelled'
Step 4: Verify gyms table has saas_subscription_id column (nullable text)
Step 5: Verify gyms table has saas_stripe_customer_id column (nullable text)
Step 6: Verify gyms table has trial_ends_at column (timestamp, nullable)
Step 7: Verify existing gyms have saas_status='trial' and trial_ends_at set

description: New gym registration automatically starts a 30-day free trial
steps: Step 1: Register a new gym via POST /gyms (or admin registration form)
Step 2: Verify newly created gym has saas_status = 'trial'
Step 3: Verify trial_ends_at is set to approximately 30 days from registration date
Step 4: Verify gym admin can log in and access all admin features during trial
Step 5: Verify no payment is required to complete registration

description: POST /admin/saas/checkout creates a Stripe Checkout session for the gym owner to subscribe to GymAccess
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: POST /admin/saas/checkout with { plan: 'monthly' }
Step 3: Verify 200 response with { url: '...' }
Step 4: Verify Stripe checkout session is created in subscription mode with GymAccess product price
Step 5: Verify session amount matches SAAS_MONTHLY_PRICE env var
Step 6: Verify success_url redirects back to admin settings
Step 7: Verify cancel_url redirects back to admin settings
Step 8: Verify customer_email is set to the gym admin's email
Step 9: POST /admin/saas/checkout with { plan: 'yearly' } and verify amount matches SAAS_YEARLY_PRICE

description: SaaS checkout.session.completed webhook activates gym's SaaS subscription
steps: Step 1: Create gym with saas_status='trial'
Step 2: POST /webhooks/stripe with checkout.session.completed event for SaaS (no 'account' field, metadata contains gymId)
Step 3: Verify gym's saas_status is updated to 'active'
Step 4: Verify gym's saas_subscription_id is stored from the Stripe subscription
Step 5: Verify gym's saas_stripe_customer_id is stored from the Stripe customer
Step 6: Verify webhook returns 200

description: Stripe webhooks handle SaaS subscription lifecycle: payment succeeded, failed, and cancelled
steps: Step 1: Set gym saas_subscription_id to a Stripe subscription ID and saas_status='active'
Step 2: POST /webhooks/stripe with invoice.payment_succeeded for the SaaS subscription (no 'account' field)
Step 3: Verify gym saas_status remains 'active'
Step 4: POST /webhooks/stripe with invoice.payment_failed for the SaaS subscription
Step 5: Verify gym saas_status is set to 'past_due'
Step 6: POST /webhooks/stripe with customer.subscription.deleted for the SaaS subscription
Step 7: Verify gym saas_status is set to 'cancelled'
Step 8: Verify all webhook calls return 200

description: POST /admin/saas/portal returns a Stripe Customer Portal URL for the gym owner to manage their SaaS subscription
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin with active SaaS subscription and saas_stripe_customer_id set
Step 2: POST /admin/saas/portal
Step 3: Verify 200 response with { url: '...' }
Step 4: Verify URL is a valid Stripe Customer Portal session URL
Step 5: Verify return_url points back to admin settings page

description: SAAS_MONTHLY_PRICE and SAAS_YEARLY_PRICE environment variables configure GymAccess subscription pricing
steps: Step 1: Set SAAS_MONTHLY_PRICE=299 and SAAS_YEARLY_PRICE=2490 in .env
Step 2: POST /admin/saas/checkout with { plan: 'monthly' }
Step 3: Verify Stripe session unit_amount = 29900 (299 NOK in øre)
Step 4: POST /admin/saas/checkout with { plan: 'yearly' }

description: Admin dashboard shows a warning banner when trial has expired and no active SaaS subscription exists
steps: Step 1: Set gym's trial_ends_at to a past date with saas_status='trial'
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Verify a prominent warning banner is displayed on the dashboard
Step 4: Verify banner text indicates the trial has expired

description: Admin billing page shows current SaaS plan, trial status, and subscription management options

Step 8: Verify current plan (monthly/yearly) is displayed
Step 9: Verify 'Manage Subscription' button is present and calls POST /admin/saas/portal
Step 10: Log in as admin with saas_status='past_due'
Step 11: Verify payment issue warning is shown with re-subscribe option

description: Stripe Connect section in admin Settings has clear visual design with status indicators
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin

description: Plan & Billing section in admin Settings has clear subscription management UI with trial countdown
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin in trial period

Step 4: Verify pricing for monthly and yearly GymAccess plans is clearly shown

Step 6: Log in as admin with active subscription
Step 7: Verify plan name, price, and next billing date are clearly presented
Step 8: Verify 'Manage Subscription' button is clearly labeled and accessible

description: Platform-level Seam API key is configured via SEAM_API_KEY environment variable
steps: Step 1: Verify SEAM_API_KEY is present in .env.example as a documented required variable
Step 2: Verify the API server reads SEAM_API_KEY on startup and exposes a Seam client utility
Step 3: Verify the Seam client is initialized from api/src/utils/seam.ts (or similar)
Step 4: Verify that if SEAM_API_KEY is not set, the igloohome integration routes return 503 with a clear error

description: Admin links their igloohome account to GymAccess via Seam Connect Webview
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin and navigate to Settings page
Step 2: Click 'Connect igloohome Account' button in the Smart Lock section
Step 3: Call POST /admin/igloohome/connect — verify it creates a Seam Connect Webview and returns a webview URL
Step 4: Verify the webview URL is opened (redirect or modal) allowing the admin to log in to their igloohome account
Step 5: After completing the Seam Connect flow, verify the gym's seam_connected_account_id is saved in the gyms table
Step 6: Verify the Settings page shows the igloohome account as 'Connected'

description: Admin can view igloohome devices linked via Seam and select one as the gym keybox
steps: Step 1: Complete Seam Connect Webview so igloohome account is linked
Step 2: Call GET /admin/igloohome/devices
Step 3: Verify response contains devices from Seam API (id, display_name, device_type) filtered to igloohome keyboxes
Step 4: In Settings UI, verify a device selector appears listing devices by name
Step 5: Select the Keybox 3 device and save
Step 6: Verify PUT /admin/settings/igloohome with { device_id: '...' } stores device_id in gyms.seam_device_id

description: Admin can disconnect igloohome integration from Settings, clearing Seam account link and device selection
steps: Step 1: Complete igloohome setup with a connected account and selected device
Step 2: Navigate to Settings page and click 'Disconnect'

description: When a member is activated and igloohome is configured, a time-bound offline algoPIN is created via Seam API
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome via Seam Connect and select a Keybox 3 device
Step 2: Create a new member and simulate Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify activateMemberAccess calls Seam API (POST /access_codes/create) with is_offline_access_code=true
Step 4: Verify the request uses starts_at = now and ends_at = subscription end date (next billing date)
Step 5: Verify the returned algoPIN code (7–9 digit number) is stored encrypted in the access_codes table
Step 6: Verify the Seam access_code_id is stored for reference (e.g. in access_codes.provider_code_id)

description: algoPIN from Seam is stored encrypted in the database and decrypted when returned via admin API
steps: Step 1: Activate a member with igloohome integration configured
Step 2: Query access_codes table directly and verify code is stored encrypted (ivHex:ciphertextHex format)
Step 3: Call GET /admin/members/:memberId/access-codes
Step 4: Verify returned code is decrypted (7–9 digit PIN)
Step 5: Verify a source or type field identifies this as an igloohome algoPIN

description: Welcome email sent to new member includes their igloohome algoPIN when igloohome integration is active
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome integration for the gym
Step 2: Activate a new member via Stripe checkout webhook
Step 3: Check MailHog (http://localhost:8025) for the welcome email
Step 4: Verify the email body contains the algoPIN code
Step 5: Verify the email body explains how to use the code on the Keybox 3
Step 6: Verify the notification_templates welcome body supports an {{access_pin}} or {{igloohome_pin}} placeholder

description: When subscription renews (invoice.payment_succeeded), a new time-bound algoPIN is created via Seam for the next billing period
steps: Step 1: Set up an active member with igloohome integration configured
Step 2: Simulate an invoice.payment_succeeded webhook for the member's subscription renewal
Step 3: Verify the API calls Seam to create a new offline access code for the next billing period
Step 4: Verify starts_at = now and ends_at = next period end date
Step 5: Verify new PIN is stored in access_codes table
Step 6: Verify payment receipt notification is sent to member containing the renewed PIN

description: When subscription is cancelled, the Seam access code is deleted and no new algoPIN is generated
steps: Step 1: Set up an active member with igloohome integration and an active algoPIN
Step 2: Simulate a customer.subscription.deleted webhook
Step 3: Verify the API calls Seam DELETE /access_codes/delete for the member's current code
Step 4: Verify member status transitions to 'cancelled'
Step 5: Verify no new PIN is created after cancellation
Step 6: Verify cancellation email mentions that keybox access has been revoked

description: When payment fails (invoice.payment_failed), the Seam access code is deleted until payment is recovered
steps: Step 1: Set up an active member with igloohome integration
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_failed webhook
Step 3: Verify member transitions to 'past_due'
Step 4: Verify the API calls Seam to delete the current access code, revoking keybox entry
Step 5: Simulate a subsequent invoice.payment_succeeded to verify a new PIN is created and member is reactivated
Step 6: Verify payment_failed notification informs member their keybox access has been suspended

description: Admin member detail page shows the igloohome algoPIN when igloohome integration is configured for the gym
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome integration for the gym
Step 2: Activate a member so an algoPIN is generated
Step 3: Navigate to the member's detail page in admin UI
Step 4: Verify the member's current igloohome algoPIN is shown (decrypted 7–9 digit number)
Step 5: Verify the PIN display has a copy-to-clipboard button
Step 6: Verify the PIN expiry date (ends_at) is shown
Step 7: Verify no igloohome PIN section appears for gyms without igloohome integration configured

description: Admin can manually regenerate an igloohome algoPIN for a member from the member detail page
steps: Step 1: Navigate to an active member's detail page with igloohome configured
Step 2: Click 'Regenerate PIN' button
Step 3: Call POST /admin/members/:memberId/igloohome/regenerate
Step 4: Verify the API deletes the existing Seam access code and creates a new one for the remaining subscription period
Step 5: Verify the new PIN is stored encrypted in access_codes
Step 6: Verify the member detail page updates to show the new PIN
Step 7: Verify a notification is optionally sent to the member with their new PIN

description: Seam/igloohome errors are handled gracefully without breaking member activation
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome integration with an invalid or missing device_id
Step 2: Trigger member activation via webhook
Step 3: Verify member is still activated (status = 'active') even if Seam PIN creation fails
Step 4: Verify the error is logged server-side
Step 5: Verify the welcome email is still sent (without a PIN if creation failed)
Step 6: Verify admin can see a warning on member detail page if PIN generation failed

description: Igloohome integration section in admin Settings has clear UI with connection status, Connect Webview button, and device selector
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Settings page with no igloohome integration configured
Step 2: Verify a clearly labeled 'Smart Lock (igloohome via Seam)' section is present
Step 3: Verify the section has a brief explanation and a 'Connect igloohome Account' button
Step 4: Verify a 'Not connected' status badge is shown
Step 5: After connecting, verify a device dropdown appears with devices listed by name
Step 6: Verify a 'Disconnect' button and 'Connected' status badge are shown when configured
Step 7: Verify the section design matches the rest of the Settings page (Nordic Forest Industrial style)

description: Member detail page igloohome algoPIN section has consistent design with copy and regenerate actions
steps: Step 1: Navigate to an active member's detail page with igloohome configured
Step 2: Verify the algoPIN is displayed in a styled code/badge element
Step 3: Verify a 'Copy PIN' button is present and functional
Step 4: Verify a 'Regenerate' button is clearly visible but styled as a secondary action
Step 5: Verify the PIN expiry date is shown alongside the PIN
Step 6: Verify the section design matches the existing access code display in style and spacing

description: Platform-level igloohome API credentials allow algoPIN generation without Seam
notes: igloohome has its own REST API (api.igloohome.co) using OAuth2 Client Credentials. GymAccess holds platform credentials; no per-lock fee. Seam is no longer required for basic algoPIN generation. Exact token URL and algoPIN endpoint to verify at developer.igloocompany.co.
steps: Step 1: Add IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_ID and IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_SECRET to .env.example as platform-level credentials
Step 2: Add IGLOOHOME_TOKEN_URL (e.g. https://auth.igloohome.co/oauth2/token) to .env.example
Step 3: Add IGLOOHOME_API_URL (e.g. https://api.igloohome.co/v2) to .env.example
Step 4: Create api/src/utils/igloohome.ts with isIgloohomeConfigured(), getAccessToken() (OAuth2 client_credentials, caches token with TTL), and createAlgoPin(lockId, startsAt, endsAt) functions
Step 5: Verify that if credentials are not set, integration routes return 503 with clear error
Step 6: Verify token is fetched with grant_type=client_credentials, client_id, client_secret, cached in memory until exp-60s

description: Admin enters their igloohome lockId in Settings to enable direct algoPIN integration (free tier)
notes: Admin finds their lockId in the igloohome app under Device Settings. No Seam Connect Webview needed. DB: add gyms.igloohome_lock_id (text, nullable) column. PUT /admin/settings already exists; extend it to accept igloohome_lock_id.
steps: Step 1: Add igloohome_lock_id column (text, nullable) to gyms table via migration
Step 2: In Settings page, add a 'Smart Lock (igloohome Direct - Free)' section separate from the Seam section
Step 3: Section shows a text input for Lock ID with helper text 'Find this in your igloohome app under Device Settings'
Step 4: Admin enters lockId and clicks Save - calls PUT /admin/settings with { igloohome_lock_id: '...' }
Step 5: Verify lockId is stored in gyms.igloohome_lock_id
Step 6: Verify Settings page reloads and shows 'Connected' status with the saved lockId
Step 7: Admin can clear the lockId to disable direct integration (sets column to NULL)

description: Member activation creates a time-bound algoPIN via igloohome direct API when igloohome_lock_id is configured
notes: Create algoPIN via POST /v2/locks/{lockId}/algopin (or /algopin/hourlybound - verify exact endpoint from igloohome docs). Body: { startDatetime, endDatetime }. Response contains the 7-9 digit PIN and a codeId for deletion. Falls back gracefully if API fails (member still activates).
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome_lock_id in gym settings and platform credentials in .env
Step 2: Activate a member via Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify activateMemberAccess calls igloohome API to create algoPIN with startsAt=now, endsAt=next billing date
Step 4: Verify the returned 7-9 digit algoPIN is stored encrypted in access_codes with source='igloohome_direct'
Step 5: Verify provider_code_id stores the igloohome codeId for future deletion
Step 6: Verify GET /admin/members/:id returns decrypted algoPIN
Step 7: Verify member still activates successfully if igloohome API call fails (graceful degradation, error logged)
Step 8: Verify welcome email contains the algoPIN

description: igloohome direct: subscription renewal creates new algoPIN for the next billing period
steps: Step 1: Set up active member with igloohome_lock_id configured (direct integration)
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_succeeded webhook for renewal
Step 3: Verify old algoPIN is deleted via igloohome API DELETE endpoint using stored provider_code_id
Step 4: Verify new algoPIN is created for next billing period via igloohome API
Step 5: Verify new PIN is stored encrypted in access_codes table with updated provider_code_id
Step 6: Verify payment receipt notification contains the new PIN

description: igloohome direct: subscription cancellation or payment failure deletes the algoPIN via igloohome API
steps: Step 1: Set up active member with igloohome direct integration and active algoPIN
Step 2: Simulate customer.subscription.deleted webhook
Step 3: Verify igloohome API DELETE is called with stored provider_code_id to revoke the PIN
Step 4: Verify access_codes record is marked invalid or removed
Step 5: Simulate invoice.payment_failed webhook for a different active member
Step 6: Verify igloohome API DELETE is called to revoke PIN during past_due transition
Step 7: Verify cancellation email informs member keybox access is revoked

description: Admin can manually regenerate igloohome direct algoPIN from member detail page
steps: Step 1: Navigate to active member detail page with igloohome direct integration configured
Step 2: Click 'Regenerate PIN' button
Step 3: Verify POST /admin/members/:id/igloohome/regenerate deletes old PIN via igloohome API
Step 4: Verify new algoPIN is created for remaining subscription period
Step 5: Verify new PIN is stored encrypted with updated provider_code_id
Step 6: Verify UI updates to show new PIN without page reload

description: Seam integration is a paid add-on: gyms table has seam_tier column ('none' or 'active'), all Seam routes require seam_tier='active'
notes: Seam charges per device/month. GymAccess wraps this as a paid add-on priced via SEAM_ADDON_PRICE env var (monthly, in cents). All existing Seam routes (/admin/igloohome/connect, /admin/igloohome/devices, /admin/igloohome/status, /admin/settings/igloohome, /admin/members/:id/igloohome/regenerate when Seam path) must check seam_tier='active' and return 402 Payment Required with a clear upgrade message otherwise.
steps: Step 1: Add seam_tier column (text, default 'none') to gyms table via migration
Step 2: Verify seam_tier accepts values: 'none', 'active'
Step 3: Verify existing gyms default to seam_tier='none' after migration
Step 4: Add SEAM_ADDON_PRICE to .env.example (monthly price in cents, e.g. 14900)
Step 5: Add seam_tier middleware check to all Seam-specific routes - return 402 { error: 'Seam integration requires upgrade', upgradeUrl: '/admin/settings' } if seam_tier != 'active'
Step 6: Verify seam_tier='active' gym can access all Seam routes normally
Step 7: Verify migration is idempotent

description: Admin can upgrade to Seam add-on via Stripe checkout from the Plan & Billing section
notes: POST /admin/saas/seam-addon creates Stripe checkout session with SEAM_ADDON_PRICE. On checkout.session.completed with addon='seam' metadata, webhook sets seam_tier='active'. Uses same SaaS customer (saas_stripe_customer_id) to attach to existing Stripe customer.
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin and navigate to Settings > Plan & Billing section
Step 2: Verify 'Smart Lock via Seam (Premium)' add-on row is listed with monthly price
Step 3: Click 'Add Seam Integration' button
Step 4: Verify POST /admin/saas/seam-addon creates a Stripe checkout session with correct price
Step 5: Simulate checkout.session.completed webhook with gymId metadata and addon='seam'
Step 6: Verify gym's seam_tier is set to 'active' in the database
Step 7: Verify Settings page now shows Seam Connect Webview section as active/unlocked
Step 8: Verify invoice.* webhooks for the Seam add-on subscription are handled correctly

description: activateMemberAccess uses igloohome direct API first, falls back to Seam if seam_tier='active', then internal PIN
notes: Priority order: (1) igloohome_direct if igloohome_lock_id is set and platform credentials are configured, (2) Seam if seam_tier='active' and seam_device_id is set, (3) internal random/shared PIN. access_codes.source field identifies which path was used.
steps: Step 1: Set igloohome_lock_id for gym (seam_tier='none') and activate member
Step 2: Verify igloohome direct API is used, access_codes.source='igloohome_direct'
Step 3: Clear igloohome_lock_id, set seam_device_id and seam_tier='active', activate member
Step 4: Verify Seam API is used, access_codes.source='igloohome'
Step 5: Clear both, activate member
Step 6: Verify fallback to internal PIN, access_codes.source='internal'
Step 7: Set both igloohome_lock_id and seam_device_id - verify direct API takes priority

description: Settings Smart Lock section shows both free (igloohome Direct) and paid (Seam) options with clear differentiation
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Settings with no integrations configured and seam_tier='none'
Step 2: Verify 'Free' badge on igloohome Direct section with lockId input field
Step 3: Verify 'Premium' badge and monthly price on Seam section with greyed-out or locked state
Step 4: Verify Seam section shows 'Upgrade' CTA button styled distinctly
Step 5: Verify igloohome Direct section has helper text explaining where to find the lockId
Step 6: Verify Seam section lists premium benefits: device auto-discovery, real-time activity logs, multi-lock support
Step 7: Switch to seam_tier='active' - verify Seam section is now fully interactive (Connect Webview button enabled)
Step 8: Section design matches Nordic Forest Industrial style

description: DB migration adds igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret columns to gyms table for per-gym credentials
notes: Each gym owner has their own igloohome account and must supply OAuth2 credentials from their igloaccess dashboard. Store per-gym instead of platform-wide env vars. Both columns are text, nullable. No encryption required for client_id; client_secret should be treated as sensitive but can be stored plaintext like other secrets in the DB (Stripe keys are stored similarly). Remove IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_ID and IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_SECRET from .env and .env.example — replace with a comment explaining credentials are now entered per-gym in Settings.
steps: Step 1: Add migration to create columns igloohome_client_id (text, nullable) and igloohome_client_secret (text, nullable) on the gyms table
Step 2: Verify migration runs on API startup without errors
Step 3: Verify existing gyms have NULL for both new columns after migration
Step 4: Remove IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_ID and IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_SECRET from .env.example and replace with a comment: '# igloohome credentials are configured per-gym in the admin Settings page'
Step 5: Verify the API starts correctly without IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_ID/SECRET in env

description: PUT /admin/settings accepts and stores per-gym igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret; GET /admin/settings returns igloohome_client_id and igloohome_configured flag but never the secret
notes: The admin API already has PUT /admin/settings for lock_id. Extend it to also accept igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret. On GET, return igloohome_client_id (so the UI can show it) and igloohome_configured: true/false (true when both client_id and client_secret are non-empty), but never return the raw client_secret. Empty string for either field should clear/null that column.
steps: Step 1: Extend PUT /admin/settings to accept igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret fields and store them in gyms table
Step 2: Verify PUT with valid client_id and client_secret saves both to DB
Step 3: Verify PUT with empty string for client_id or client_secret sets the column to NULL
Step 4: Extend GET /admin/settings response to include igloohome_client_id and igloohome_configured (boolean: true when both columns are non-null and non-empty)
Step 5: Verify GET never includes igloohome_client_secret in the response
Step 6: Verify gym isolation — each gym only sees and updates its own credentials

description: activateMemberAccess reads igloohome credentials from the gyms table (per-gym) instead of global env vars
notes: isIgloohomeConfigured() must be updated to accept the gym's client_id and client_secret from the DB row rather than reading process.env. getAccessToken() and createAlgoPin() must accept clientId and clientSecret as parameters. The activation flow already queries the gym record — just pass igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret from the result. The in-memory token cache should be keyed by clientId to avoid cross-gym token collisions.
steps: Step 1: Update isIgloohomeConfigured() in igloohome.ts to accept (clientId, clientSecret) parameters instead of reading env vars
Step 2: Update getAccessToken() to accept (clientId, clientSecret) parameters and key the token cache by clientId
Step 3: Update createAlgoPin() to accept and forward (clientId, clientSecret) to getAccessToken()
Step 4: Update activateMemberAccess() in subscriptions.ts to pass member.igloohome_client_id and member.igloohome_client_secret to isIgloohomeConfigured() and createAlgoPin()
Step 5: Activate a member for a gym with igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret set in the DB — verify access_codes.source = 'igloohome_direct' and a valid algoPIN is generated
Step 6: Verify that a gym without credentials set falls back to internal PIN (source = 'internal')
Step 7: Verify no IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_ID or IGLOOHOME_CLIENT_SECRET env var is read anywhere in the igloohome activation path

description: Settings page has input fields for igloohome Client ID and Client Secret alongside Lock ID; secret field is masked and shows a 'configured' indicator after saving
notes: The existing igloohome Direct section in Settings already has a Lock ID input. Add two more fields: Client ID (text input, value pre-filled from GET /admin/settings igloohome_client_id) and Client Secret (password input, always blank on load since the API never returns it — show a 'Configured' badge/indicator when igloohome_configured=true). All three fields (Lock ID, Client ID, Client Secret) are saved together with the existing Save Lock ID button (rename to 'Save igloohome Settings'). Clearing all three and saving disables the integration.
steps: Step 1: Add 'Client ID' text input field to the igloohome Direct section in Settings, pre-filled with igloohome_client_id from GET /admin/settings
Step 2: Add 'Client Secret' password input field — always starts blank on page load
Step 3: When igloohome_configured=true (from GET response), show a green 'Configured' badge next to the Client Secret field indicating a secret is already saved
Step 4: When igloohome_configured=false, show no badge and Client Secret field is required to enable the integration
Step 5: Rename the save button to 'Save igloohome Settings' and have it submit Lock ID, Client ID, and Client Secret together via PUT /admin/settings

Step 6: If Client Secret field is left blank on save, do not overwrite the existing secret in the DB (send a sentinel value or omit the field — API should only update client_secret if a non-empty value is provided)
Step 7: After successful save with all three fields, UI shows 'Lock ID configured' and 'Configured' badge on Client Secret
Step 8: Admin can clear the integration by emptying all fields and saving

description: igloohome per-gym credential deletion and renewal paths use gym's own credentials from DB
notes: The algoPIN deletion path (subscription cancellation, payment failure, manual regenerate) also calls the igloohome API with deleteAlgoPin(). This must also use per-gym credentials from the gyms table, not env vars. The revokeMemberAccess() and regenerate PIN routes must query the gym's igloohome_client_id and igloohome_client_secret and pass them to deleteAlgoPin().
steps: Step 1: Update deleteAlgoPin() in igloohome.ts to accept (clientId, clientSecret, lockId, pinId) instead of reading env vars
Step 2: Update revokeMemberAccess() or equivalent cancellation/suspension logic to query gym credentials and pass to deleteAlgoPin()
Step 3: Update POST /admin/members/:id/igloohome/regenerate to query gym credentials and use them for both deletion of old PIN and creation of new PIN
Step 4: Cancel a subscription for a member with igloohome direct active — verify the API calls igloohome DELETE with the gym's own credentials
Step 5: Manually regenerate a PIN via the admin UI — verify old PIN is deleted and new PIN created using per-gym credentials
Step 6: Verify graceful degradation: if gym credentials are missing or the igloohome API call fails, the member status update still completes and the error is logged

description: Landing page hero clearly targets gym owners with problem-first headline and owner-focused CTAs
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:3000

description: POST /access/request-pin endpoint generates an on-demand igloohome AlgoPIN for an active member, authenticated via manage token
notes: New API endpoint for on-demand PIN generation. Authenticated via the member's manage_token (same token used for /manage/[token] page). The endpoint verifies: (1) valid manage token, (2) member status is 'active', (3) gym has igloohome configured. On success, calls igloohome API to create an AlgoPIN with a 2-hour validity window, stores it in access_codes with source='igloohome_direct', and returns the PIN and validity time.
steps: Step 1: Call POST /access/request-pin with valid manage_token for an active member with igloohome configured
Step 2: Verify response contains { pin: '...', valid_until: '...' } with a 2-hour validity window
Step 3: Verify the PIN is a 7-9 digit number from the igloohome API
Step 4: Verify the PIN is stored encrypted in access_codes table with source='igloohome_direct'
Step 5: Verify access_codes record has valid_from=now and valid_to=now+2hours
Step 6: Call POST /access/request-pin with invalid manage_token — verify 401 Unauthorized
Step 7: Call POST /access/request-pin for a cancelled/expired member — verify 403 with message 'Membership must be active'
Step 8: Call POST /access/request-pin for a member whose gym has no igloohome configured — verify 404 or appropriate error

description: On-demand PIN reuse: if a PIN was generated for the member less than 2 hours ago, the existing PIN is returned instead of generating a new one
notes: Before calling the igloohome API, the endpoint checks access_codes for a recent PIN (source='igloohome_direct', created within last 2 hours, valid_to still in the future). If found, returns the existing PIN (decrypted) and its valid_until time. This prevents unnecessary PIN generation and avoids hitting lock storage limits.
steps: Step 1: Call POST /access/request-pin for an active member — a new PIN is generated
Step 2: Call POST /access/request-pin again immediately for the same member
Step 3: Verify the same PIN is returned (not a new one)
Step 4: Verify no new igloohome API call was made (same access_codes record reused)
Step 5: Verify the valid_until time matches the original PIN's expiry
Step 6: Wait until the PIN is older than 2 hours (or simulate with DB update)
Step 7: Call POST /access/request-pin again — verify a new PIN is generated via igloohome API
Step 8: Verify the old access_code record is superseded and a new one is created

description: On-demand PIN rate limiting: maximum 3 new PIN generations per hour per member to prevent abuse
notes: Rate limit applies to actual new PIN generations (not reuses of existing PINs). Count new access_codes with source='igloohome_direct' created in the last hour for the member. If >= 3, return 429 Too Many Requests with a message indicating when the member can request a new PIN. Reuse of existing PINs (within 2-hour window) does not count toward the rate limit.
steps: Step 1: Generate 3 new PINs within 1 hour for the same member (may need to expire each before requesting next)
Step 2: Request a 4th PIN — verify 429 Too Many Requests response
Step 3: Verify the error message indicates when the member can request a new PIN
Step 4: Verify that reusing an existing PIN (within 2-hour window) does NOT count toward the rate limit
Step 5: Wait until the oldest PIN generation is more than 1 hour old
Step 6: Verify a new PIN can be generated again after the rate limit window passes

description: On-demand PIN is generated with a short 2-hour validity window via igloohome AlgoPIN API
notes: When creating a new AlgoPIN, set the validity window to 2 hours from the current time. Use the igloohome hourly AlgoPIN endpoint (POST /devices/{lockId}/algopin/hourly). The short window ensures the PIN is used immediately (solving the AlgoPIN activation constraint) and expires quickly.
steps: Step 1: Request a new PIN via POST /access/request-pin
Step 2: Verify the igloohome API is called with startDatetime=now and endDatetime=now+2hours
Step 3: Verify the access_codes record has valid_from and valid_to matching the 2-hour window
Step 4: Verify the PIN response includes valid_until timestamp in ISO 8601 format
Step 5: Verify the PIN naturally expires after 2 hours (no manual deletion needed)

The PIN should be easy to read (large, spaced digits). Include a countdown or time remaining indicator. The display persists until the page is refreshed or the PIN expires.

Step 2: Verify the PIN is displayed in large, readable format (e.g., 'Door PIN: 483920')

description: Bluetooth self-invite: member can onboard to igloohome Bluetooth access from their personal management page
notes: Primary access method. The 'Unlock with Bluetooth' button initiates a flow for the member to set up Bluetooth access via the igloohome mobile app. This involves generating a share/invite link via the igloohome API that allows the member to add the lock to their igloohome app. Once onboarded, the member can unlock directly via Bluetooth without needing a PIN. The exact igloohome API for user invitation/sharing needs to be verified at developer.igloocompany.co.
steps: Step 1: Press 'Unlock with Bluetooth' on the member page for an active member
Step 2: Verify the system initiates Bluetooth onboarding (e.g., generates an igloohome share/invite link)
Step 3: Verify the member receives instructions for setting up Bluetooth access via the igloohome app
Step 4: Verify the invite is scoped to the gym's lock only
Step 5: Verify only active members can initiate Bluetooth onboarding

description: Remove automatic igloohome PIN generation from member activation flow (checkout.session.completed webhook)
notes: The current activateMemberAccess() function creates an igloohome AlgoPIN on member activation. With the new on-demand strategy, PINs are no longer generated automatically when a member is activated. The activation flow should still: (1) set member status to active, (2) send welcome email, (3) generate internal PIN if igloohome is NOT configured. But it should NOT call igloohome API to create an AlgoPIN. The welcome email should direct the member to their personal page for access.
steps: Step 1: Configure igloohome for a gym (client_id, client_secret, lock_id)
Step 2: Activate a new member via checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify member status is set to 'active'
Step 4: Verify NO igloohome API call is made to create an AlgoPIN
Step 5: Verify NO access_codes record with source='igloohome_direct' is created during activation
Step 6: Verify welcome email is sent but does NOT contain an AlgoPIN
Step 7: Verify welcome email contains a link to the member's personal management page for accessing the gym
Step 8: Verify that for gyms WITHOUT igloohome, internal PIN generation during activation still works as before

description: Remove automatic igloohome PIN regeneration from subscription renewal webhook (invoice.payment_succeeded)
notes: The current renewal handler creates a new AlgoPIN for the next billing period and deletes the old one. With on-demand PINs, this is no longer needed. The renewal webhook should still update subscription dates and send payment receipt, but should NOT call igloohome API. On-demand PINs with 2-hour validity expire naturally.
steps: Step 1: Set up an active member with igloohome configured
Step 2: Trigger invoice.payment_succeeded webhook for subscription renewal
Step 3: Verify subscription dates are updated correctly
Step 4: Verify NO igloohome API call is made (no new AlgoPIN created, no old PIN deleted)
Step 5: Verify payment receipt notification is still sent
Step 6: Verify member can still request on-demand PINs after renewal

description: Remove automatic igloohome PIN deletion from cancellation and payment failure webhooks
notes: The current handlers call igloohome API DELETE to revoke PINs on cancellation (customer.subscription.deleted) and payment failure (invoice.payment_failed). With short-lived on-demand PINs (2-hour validity), PINs expire naturally. The webhooks should still update member status and send notifications, but should NOT call igloohome API to delete PINs. Access control is enforced by the on-demand endpoint checking membership status before generating new PINs.
steps: Step 1: Set up an active member with igloohome configured who has a recently generated on-demand PIN
Step 2: Trigger customer.subscription.deleted webhook
Step 3: Verify member status is updated to 'cancelled'
Step 4: Verify NO igloohome API DELETE call is made
Step 5: Verify cancellation email is sent
Step 6: Verify the member can no longer request new PINs (POST /access/request-pin returns 403)
Step 7: Trigger invoice.payment_failed for another active member
Step 8: Verify member status is updated to 'past_due'
Step 9: Verify NO igloohome API DELETE call is made for payment failure
Step 10: Verify past_due members cannot request new on-demand PINs

description: On-demand PIN generation uses per-gym igloohome credentials from the gyms table
notes: The POST /access/request-pin endpoint must look up the gym's igloohome_client_id, igloohome_client_secret, and igloohome_lock_id from the gyms table. It uses these per-gym credentials to authenticate with the igloohome API and generate the AlgoPIN. The token cache should be keyed by client_id to avoid cross-gym collisions.
steps: Step 1: Configure gym A with igloohome credentials (client_id_A, secret_A, lock_id_A)
Step 2: Configure gym B with different igloohome credentials (client_id_B, secret_B, lock_id_B)

Step 4: Request a PIN for a member of gym B — verify igloohome API is called with gym B's credentials
Step 5: Verify token cache maintains separate tokens per gym (keyed by client_id)
Step 6: Verify if gym credentials are missing/incomplete, the endpoint returns an appropriate error

description: On-demand PIN generation handles igloohome API errors gracefully with clear user-facing error messages
notes: If the igloohome API call fails (network error, auth error, rate limit, invalid lock_id), the endpoint should return a clear error message to the member. Errors should be logged for admin visibility. The member's status should not be affected by PIN generation failures.

Step 2: Request a PIN when igloohome API returns a network timeout — verify clear error message
Step 3: Request a PIN with an invalid lock_id — verify clear error message
Step 4: Verify all igloohome API errors are logged with sufficient detail for debugging
Step 5: Verify the member's active status is not affected by any PIN generation failure
